content.problemStatement: Chef sometimes suffers from insomnia. During sleepless nights, he often plays this game: He creates an array **A** of **N** integers and also decides one integer **K**. After that, he tries to count the number of non-empty contiguous subsequences (subsegments) of **A**, such that there are no bad pairs of integers in this subsegment. A pair **(x, y)** of integers is called bad if **x** is situated to the left of **y** in the array and **x mod y = K**. Finally, before going to sleep, Chef wrote down the answer on a sheet of paper. Sadly, he has forgotten it this morning and want your help regarding that.
content.inputFormat: The first line contains two integers - N and K. The second line contains N integers - A1, A2 ... AN - separated by spaces, denoting the array A.
content.outputFormat: A single line containing an integer - the answer to the problem.

solution.approach: To solve this problem, we can use dynamic programming. We will maintain a DP array where dp[i] will store the number of good subarrays ending at index i. We will iterate over the array and for each element, we will check all previous elements. If the current element is not divisible by any of the previous elements with a remainder of K, we will update dp[i] accordingly.
solution.timeComplexity: O(N^2) - because for each element, we are checking all previous elements.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(N) - because we are using a DP array of size N.

code.Python3:
import sys
N, K = map(int, input().split())
A = list(map(int, input().split()))
dp = [1] * N
ans = 1
for i in range(1, N):
    for j in range(i):
        if A[j] % A[i] == K:
            dp[i] = dp[i] + dp[j]
    ans = ans + dp[i]
print(ans)
